title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Benign Prostatic Hypertrophy
intro: Herbal teas for Benign prostatic hypertrophy are a type of herbal remedy used to alleviate the symptoms of an enlarged prostate, a non-cancerous condition that affects millions of men worldwide. These herbal teas can treat this ailment by reducing inflammation, improving urinary flow, and relaxing the muscles in the prostate gland, thus providing relief from frequent urination and other uncomfortable symptoms. Some popular herbal teas used to treat Benign prostatic hypertrophy include Saw Palmetto, which blocks the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone, a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement; Pygeum, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve prostate function; and Urinary System tea, which includes herbs like Juniper berries and Parsley to increase urine production and reduce swelling; and Cinnamon tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the prostate. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Dandelion root, Green tea, and Turmeric tea have also been found to be beneficial in reducing symptoms associated with Benign prostatic hypertrophy.

preparations.herb_desc: Serenoa repens teas, also known as saw palmetto teas, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), a condition characterized by an enlarged prostate gland. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, including urinary frequency and nocturia. The bioactive constituents of Serenoa repens, including phytosterols, fatty acids, and terpenoids, have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement. By reducing DHT levels and alleviating inflammation, Serenoa repens teas provide a natural and effective treatment option for BPH, improving urinary flow and overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Serenoa repens (Saw Palmetto) leaves and flowers. You can buy this at a health food store or online.
Combine the dried Serenoa repens with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Some people prefer their tea sweet or sour.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, as directed by a healthcare professional or as needed for symptom relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study in Clinical therapeutics, Serenoa repens teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy are useful in treatment, but they do not act by changing hormone levels in the body.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Serenoa repens teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but potential side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps. Precautions to take when using Serenoa repens teas include avoiding intake with other medications, especially blood thinners and certain antidepressants, and being cautious of interactions with other herbal supplements, as well as monitoring for allergic reactions such as hives or itching.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Serenoa repens tea for BPH
Drink 2 cups of Serenoa repens tea per day, divided into 2 doses
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Serenoa repens per cup of boiling water
Use Serenoa repens tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable improvements
Monitor your symptoms and adjust your dosage as recommended by your doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been explored as a potential natural remedy for treating benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), a common condition affecting older men. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcuminoids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate symptoms of BPH. These compounds may inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, thus providing relief from urinary retention and other associated symptoms. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat BPH include its natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and potential improvement in overall prostate health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/2 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder.
Boil the water in a pot.
Turn off the heat and add the Curcuma longa root powder to the water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals, for relief from benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Curcuma longa teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy show potential benefits as certain phytocompounds, including curcumin, have been reported to be useful for the management of BPH.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can potentially alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, diarrhea, and headache in some individuals. Precautions when using Curcuma longa teas for this ailment include monitoring blood sugar levels, as it may lower blood sugar, and being cautious when taking anticoagulant medications, as it may increase the risk of bleeding.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for BPH.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa in 1 cup of hot water.
Drink Curcuma longa tea 2 times a day, after breakfast and dinner.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons after 2 weeks if needed.
Continue for 3-6 months and monitor symptoms for improvement.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and spasmolytic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, thereby alleviating symptoms such as urinary frequency, urgency, and nocturia associated with BPH. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale teas, including gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to relax smooth muscle cells in the prostate gland, improving urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat BPH include reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and a natural alternative to pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Zingiber officinale (Ginger root) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Zingiber officinale to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the ginger root.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day for relief from benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal discomfort, such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset, as well as potential interactions with blood thinning medications. Precautions should be taken when consuming Zingiber officinale teas for this condition, including avoiding excessive consumption, which may exacerbate stomach issues and potentially worsen symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 2-3 teaspoons of Zingiber officinale powder per cup.
Use 250-300 ml of boiling water to make each cup.
Drink tea for 6-12 weeks to see noticeable improvements.
Combine with a healthy diet and regular exercise for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to improve blood flow to the prostate gland, reducing inflammation and swelling associated with BPH. By improving microcirculation and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba teas may help alleviate symptoms of BPH, such as urinary frequency and weak urine flow. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat BPH include reduced risk of prostate enlargement, improved urinary function, and a decrease in the need for surgical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup to remove the Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat the symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas for this ailment include starting with low doses, monitoring blood sugar levels, and being aware of potential interactions with other herbal supplements and medications to avoid exacerbating the condition.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for BPH
Start with 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 3-4 weeks
Monitor urinary symptoms and adjust tea frequency as needed
Use 2-4% Ginkgo biloba extract, 160-240 mg per serving, daily
Combine Ginkgo biloba tea with a balanced diet and exercise routine

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, derived from the leaves of the stinging nettle plant, have been used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The herbal preparation's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH, helps to alleviate symptoms such as enlarged prostate and urinary frequency. Bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids in Urtica dioica teas may help to reduce the size of the prostate gland and improve urine flow, providing relief from BPH symptoms. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat BPH include reduced prostate size, improved urinary function, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with BPH.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers, or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers.
Combine the Urtica dioica with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Planta medica", Urtica dioica teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy showed the 20% methanolic extract to be the most effective with a 51.4% inhibition of induced growth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions when using this herbal preparation include avoiding it if you have allergies to plants in the nettle family, as well as being cautious when combining it with other medications due to potential interactions.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Urtica dioica tea for BPH.
Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2 times a day, after meals.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup, adjust as needed.
Continue for 3-6 months, then reassess with a healthcare professional.
Monitor urine flow and prostate size with regular check-ups.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These properties help to alleviate the symptoms of BPH by reducing inflammation and swelling in the prostate gland, improving urinary flow, and relieving symptoms such as frequent urination and nocturia. The bioactive constituents of Equisetum arvense teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which help to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce the size of the prostate gland. The benefits of using Equisetum arvense teas to treat BPH include reduced symptoms, improved urinary function, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with BPH, making it a promising herbal remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense (horsetail) leaves and stems.
Wash the horsetail in cold water, then pat it dry.
Use a coffee grinder or herb grinder to finely chop 2 tablespoons of the dried or 1 cup of fresh horsetail.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped horsetail in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain and drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day for desired effect.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but its use may be associated with possible side effects such as diarrhea, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including monitoring for interactions with other medications and adjusting the dosage to avoid exacerbating urinary retention issues.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Equisetum arvense tea for BPH.
Drink 1/2 cup of Equisetum arvense tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Equisetum arvense per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water as needed.
Continue usage for 3-6 months under doctor's supervision.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been explored as a potential treatment for benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their diuretic and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. The herbal preparation's ability to reduce inflammation and improve urinary flow makes it a promising natural remedy for BPH sufferers. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects on BPH. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas may help to alleviate symptoms of BPH, including urinary frequency, nocturia, and decreased urine flow, offering a natural alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 2-3 weeks to help alleviate benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and stomach cramps, as well as interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and antacids. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include starting with small doses, monitoring blood pressure and heart rate, and being cautious when combining it with other herbal remedies or medications that may have synergistic effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Rosmarinus officinalis teas for BPH.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the leaves.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week, for 3-6 months.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust your dosage under your doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper nigrum teas have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Piper nigrum, such as piperine, piperonal, and limonene, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with BPH. These teas are believed to help treat BPH by reducing the size of the prostate gland, improving urine flow, and alleviating symptoms such as urinary frequency and nocturia. The benefits of Piper nigrum teas in treating BPH include improved urinary function, reduced symptoms, and a potential reduction in the risk of complications associated with the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Piper nigrum (black pepper) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried Piper nigrum to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the Piper nigrum.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, for relief from benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Piper nigrum teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions or interactions with other medications that could exacerbate the condition.

Precautions should be taken to avoid using Piper nigrum teas in excessive amounts, as high doses may lead to increased heart rate and blood pressure, and to monitor for signs of kidney or liver damage, which are rare but potential complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, a traditional herbal preparation, have been studied for their potential to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, such as urinary frequency and nocturia. Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been found to relax the smooth muscles of the prostate and urinary tract, thereby improving urine flow and reducing the risk of urinary retention. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat BPH include improved urinary function, reduced symptoms, and a decrease in the need for pharmaceutical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea strainer.
Measure 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and place it in the tea strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the tea strainer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the tea strainer with the Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help manage symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but its use may lead to side effects such as increased blood pressure, potassium deficiency, and edema due to the spironolactone-like effect of glycyrrhizin, which can interfere with the body's natural potassium balance. To minimize the risk of side effects, it is essential to consume Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in moderation, preferably under 200mg of glycyrrhizin per day, and avoid using it for extended periods or in combination with other diuretics or potassium-depleting substances.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The silymarin complex, a bioactive constituent of Silybum marianum, helps to inhibit the growth of prostate cells, reduce inflammation, and improve urine flow. The flavonoids and polyphenols present in this herbal preparation also have a protective effect on the prostate gland by reducing oxidative stress and promoting cellular health. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum teas may help alleviate BPH symptoms, improve prostate health, and enhance overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum (milk thistle) flowers and leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the 1 tablespoon of Silybum marianum to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
preparations.preparation_study: According to BMC complementary and alternative medicine, Silybum marianum teas are not mentioned in the study provided as a treatment for benign prostatic hypertrophy, but other plants such as Juglans regia, Quercus infectoria, and Sambucus ebulus were used for this condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions when using Silybum marianum teas include monitoring for signs of kidney or liver damage, and being aware of potential interactions with blood thinners and other medications, as well as avoiding use if pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Cucumis sativus teas, also known as cucumber tea, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea helps to treat this ailment by reducing the size of the prostate and alleviating symptoms such as frequent urination and nocturia. The bioactive constituents of Cucumis sativus, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, are responsible for its medicinal effects. The benefits of consuming Cucumis sativus teas for BPH treatment include improved urinary function, reduced symptoms of BPH, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cucumis sativus leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea strainer.
Wash the Cucumis sativus leaves in cold water and pat dry with a paper towel.
Boil the water in a pot and add the Cucumis sativus leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a tea strainer into a cup. Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Cucumis sativus teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy were not mentioned, but other plants such as Juglans regia, Quercus infectoria, Sambucus ebulus, and Zea mays were used for this condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cucumis sativus teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor urine output and blood pressure, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, especially if you have kidney or heart problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, made from the oat plant, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents, including avenanthramides and avenalin, help to reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscles in the prostate gland, thereby alleviating the symptoms of BPH. By promoting urine flow and reducing the size of the prostate gland, Avena sativa teas provide relief from the symptoms of BPH, including urinary frequency, urgency, and nocturia. The benefits of Avena sativa teas in treating BPH include reduced inflammation, improved urinary function, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with BPH.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the Avena sativa in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for optimal results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Drugs & aging, Avena sativa teas is not mentioned in the provided study as an effective treatment for benign prostatic hypertrophy, but Serenoa repens (saw palmetto) is mentioned as an effective option.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but potential side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Avena sativa teas in large quantities, as this may lead to increased sedation and interaction with other medications, particularly those used to treat high blood pressure and depression.

preparations.herb_desc: Tribulus terrestris teas have been traditionally used to treat the symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), a non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland. The bioactive constituents present in Tribulus terrestris teas, such as flavonoids and saponins, are believed to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and alleviate symptoms like frequent urination and prostate pain. The saponins in this herbal preparation have been shown to have diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve BPH symptoms by promoting urination and reducing swelling in the prostate area. Regular consumption of Tribulus terrestris teas may also help to improve prostate health and alleviate symptoms of BPH, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Get 2 cups of water and bring it to a boil.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Tribulus terrestris root powder into a teapot.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the powder in the teapot.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The journal of sexual medicine, Tribulus terrestris teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy do not have any information in the given study, as the study focuses on female sexual dysfunction and its treatment, and does not mention benign prostatic hypertrophy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Tribulus terrestris teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include increased urination, mild stomach upset, and temporary changes in blood pressure. When using Tribulus terrestris teas to treat this ailment, precautions should be taken to avoid consuming excessive amounts, especially for individuals with high blood pressure or cardiovascular issues, as it may worsen these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Prunus africana teas have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), a non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat this ailment include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the size of the prostate gland and alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency and obstruction. The bioactive constituents of Prunus africana teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which include inhibiting 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme involved in the production of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to the development of BPH. The benefits of using Prunus africana teas to treat BPH include reduced symptoms, improved urinary flow, and a decrease in the risk of complications such as urinary retention and bladder stones.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Prunus africana leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add honey to taste and stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, before meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Prunus africana teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy have been found to be effective, but an alternative option, Prunus domestica, which contains a higher amount of beta-sitosterol, has shown comparable efficacy and can be used as a potential backup for the management of BPH.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Prunus africana teas can help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and changes in blood pressure, as well as interactions with other medications such as blood thinners and diuretics. To use Prunus africana teas safely, one should start with small doses, gradually increase as needed, and avoid consuming it with other stimulants or medications that may exacerbate its effects, and be cautious when taking it with other herbal preparations that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Pygeum africanum teas have been traditionally used to treat the symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), a non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland. The herbal preparation's properties, including its ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate growth, help to alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination and nocturia. The bioactive constituents of Pygeum africanum, including ferulic acid, isoflavones, and phytosterols, are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic effects, which include reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow. The benefits of using Pygeum africanum teas to treat BPH include improved quality of life, reduced symptoms, and a lower risk of complications such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones.

Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally 30 minutes after meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The Annals of pharmacotherapy, Pygeum africanum teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy are among the botanical therapies used in the treatment of BPH, particularly in stages I and II, based on available information.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Pygeum africanum teas can potentially cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions when used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, in addition to interactions with certain medications. It is recommended to avoid taking Pygeum africanum teas if you are pregnant or breastfeeding, and to monitor your liver function while using this herbal preparation to treat this ailment.

Regular consumption of Vitex agnus-castus teas may also reduce the risk of complications associated with BPH, such as urinary retention and kidney damage, making it a natural and potentially beneficial treatment option.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Remove the pot from heat and add the dried flowers.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for desired effect.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitex agnus-castus teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its interaction with other medications and its ability to slow down the heart rate. Precautions should be taken when using Vitex agnus-castus teas, such as avoiding high doses and combining it with other herbs that have a sedative effect to prevent excessive drowsiness.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax quinquefolius teas, derived from the American ginseng plant, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including ginsenosides and polysaccharides, help to reduce the size of the prostate and alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency and urgency. The ginsenosides, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cancer cells and reduce the production of androgens, which contribute to the development of BPH. Regular consumption of Panax quinquefolius teas has been reported to provide relief from BPH symptoms, improve urinary flow, and reduce the risk of complications associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Panax quinquefolius root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Put the dried root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the dried root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Drugs & aging, Panax quinquefolius teas have not been mentioned in this study as a treatment for benign prostatic hypertrophy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax quinquefolius teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but potential side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach discomfort, as well as allergic reactions and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Panax quinquefolius teas in large quantities or for extended periods, as this may exacerbate symptoms or lead to adverse reactions, and individuals with bleeding disorders or taking anticoagulant medications should exercise caution.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been studied for their potential benefits in treating benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), a non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides and ginsenol, are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects, as they may help to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and modulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to BPH. The properties of Panax ginseng teas, such as their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, may also help to alleviate symptoms of BPH, including urinary frequency and difficulty, by promoting prostate health and reducing oxidative stress. By incorporating Panax ginseng teas into their regimen, individuals with BPH may experience improved urinary function and overall prostate health, making it a promising natural remedy for this common condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil the water in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour it into the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root to the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginseng root. Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the Panax ginseng tea 2-3 times a day for 2-3 months to help alleviate benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Andrologia, Panax ginseng teas have been studied for their potential effects on benign prostatic hypertrophy, but there is no specific information provided in the given study about their effects on this condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but it may cause side effects such as insomnia, anxiety, and headaches due to its stimulatory properties. Precautions should be taken when using Panax ginseng teas, including monitoring blood pressure and sugar levels, as it may interact with certain medications and exacerbate underlying conditions like hypertension and diabetes.

preparations.herb_desc: Ruscus aculeatus teas, a herbal preparation derived from the Ruscus aculeatus plant, have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, help to reduce the size of the prostate gland, alleviate urinary retention, and alleviate symptoms such as nocturia and urinary frequency. The saponins in Ruscus aculeatus teas, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone, a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement. By reducing the size of the prostate gland and alleviating symptoms, Ruscus aculeatus teas provide relief for individuals suffering from BPH, improving their overall quality of life and urinary function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ruscus aculeatus leaves. Wash them with clean water.
Dry the leaves completely. You can air-dry them or use a food dehydrator.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried Ruscus aculeatus leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down.
Drink 1/2 cup of the cooled tea 2-3 times a day for relief from benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ruscus aculeatus teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include being aware of potential interactions with other medications, avoiding use if pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitoring blood pressure and kidney function closely, as excessive consumption may worsen underlying conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosa canina teas have been traditionally used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) due to their potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents present in Rosa canina teas, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, may help to reduce prostate size, alleviate symptoms of BPH, and improve urinary flow. The anti-inflammatory properties of these bioactive constituents may also help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the prostate gland, thereby providing relief from BPH symptoms. Regular consumption of Rosa canina teas may offer benefits in alleviating BPH symptoms, improving urinary health, and reducing the risk of complications associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Rosa canina flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Combine the strained liquid with 1 cup of cold water and 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to see improvements.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosa canina teas can be used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea or stomach upset, and interactions with other medications. When using Rosa canina teas for this ailment, precautions should be taken to avoid over-consumption, which may exacerbate symptoms, and to monitor blood pressure and kidney function, as some individuals may experience increased blood pressure or impaired kidney function.

preparation_study: According to The Annals of pharmacotherapy, Serenoa repens teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy have shown efficacy in treating a number of objective and subjective symptoms in patients with BPH, stages I and II.
intro_study: According to the review of herbal therapies in "The Annals of pharmacotherapy", teas containing herbs such as Serenoa repens, Pygeum africanum, Urtica dioica radix, and Cucurbita peponis semen may be beneficial in treating some symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy, particularly for stages I and II.
